content_en:
The body of Mizan al-I'tidal consists of individual narrator entries that demonstrate al-Dhahabi's method in practice. Examining several representative cases illustrates how he weighs the evidence for and against narrators whose hadith transmissions appear in the major collections.

The Case of Abu al-Zubayr al-Makki

Abu al-Zubayr Muhammad ibn Muslim al-Makki (d. ca. 128 AH) is one of the most significant figures discussed in Mizan al-I'tidal. He was a major transmitter of hadith in Makkah, a student of the Companion Jabir ibn Abdallah and many other important early narrators. His reliability was challenged by Shu'ba ibn al-Hajjaj, who witnessed him accepting a bribe and adjusting his narration accordingly. This accusation, coming from one of the most authoritative hadith critics of the second century, created a lasting uncertainty about Abu al-Zubayr's reliability.

Al-Dhahabi presents the evidence carefully: Abu al-Zubayr was criticized by Shu'ba and some others, but defended by Yahya ibn Maeen and others who considered him trustworthy. Al-Dhahabi's conclusion is that Abu al-Zubayr is acceptable when his student Layth ibn Saad specifies that he heard the hadith directly from Jabir — a specification that removes the concern that Abu al-Zubayr was selective in his transmission based on which hadiths earned him better treatment. Without this specification, his transmissions from Jabir should be treated with caution.

Fabricators and Their Recognition

Mizan al-I'tidal contains entries for a number of narrators whom al-Dhahabi considers to be outright fabricators of hadith. These are the most severe cases in his work, and he treats them with corresponding seriousness. He discusses the signs by which fabricated hadiths were identified: unusual content that contradicts well-established Sunnah, narratives that serve obvious sectarian interests, chains of transmission that are impossible given the dates and locations of the narrators, and specific reports about fabricators caught in the act of inventing transmissions.

Al-Dhahabi is careful to distinguish between fabricators of complete hadiths (the most serious category) and narrators who occasionally made errors, confused narrations, or transmitted weak reports in good faith. The former are to be completely avoided; the latter are to be treated with appropriate caution depending on the degree and pattern of their errors.

Theological Sectarians as Narrators

A significant methodological challenge addressed in Mizan al-I'tidal is the status of narrators who held heterodox theological positions — Mutazilites, Qadarites, Murjites, and various degrees of Shia affiliation. Al-Dhahabi's approach here is notably balanced: he does not automatically disqualify narrators because of their theological views, but he does consider whether their views were of a kind likely to lead them to distort hadith in favor of their theological positions.

His most nuanced discussions are of Shia narrators, whom he categorizes carefully. A narrator with mild Shia sympathies (preference for Ali over other Companions in political matters) does not have those sympathies affect his reliability in transmitting hadiths about worship, transactions, or other non-political matters. A narrator whose Shia commitments extend to fabricating reports that glorify Ali or denigrate other Companions is in an entirely different category. Al-Dhahabi's careful distinction between these categories represented an advance in the sophistication of narrator criticism.
title_id: Perawi-Perawi yang Dikritik Terkemuka: Studi Kasus
content_id:
Isi dari Mizan al-I'tidal terdiri dari entri-entri perawi individual yang menunjukkan metode al-Dzahabi dalam praktik. Memeriksa beberapa kasus yang representatif menggambarkan bagaimana beliau menimbang bukti-bukti dan sampai pada penilaian-penilaiannya. Setiap entri mengikuti pola yang umumnya konsisten: identifikasi perawi, pengumpulan penilaian-penilaian dari para kritikus sebelumnya, dan penilaian al-Dzahabi sendiri tentang status perawi.

Kasus-kasus di mana al-Dzahabi tidak setuju dengan penilaian-penilaian para kritikus sebelumnya sangat instruktif. Dalam beberapa kasus, beliau berpendapat bahwa kritik telah terlalu keras — bahwa seorang perawi yang ditandai sebagai lemah oleh satu atau dua kritikus sebenarnya menunjukkan transmisi yang dapat diterima ketika keseluruhan bukti diperiksa. Dalam kasus-kasus lain, beliau berpendapat bahwa pujian yang diberikan oleh beberapa otoritas tidak sebanding dengan masalah-masalah yang dicatat oleh kritikus-kritikus lain, dan bahwa transmisi perawi harus diperlakukan dengan kehati-hatian.

Pertanyaan tentang pengaruh teologis dan afiliasi sektarian pada evaluasi perawi muncul sepanjang Mizan al-I'tidal. Al-Dzahabi menangani perawi-perawi dari berbagai latar belakang teologis — Mu'tazili, Syiah, Khawarij, dan lain-lain — dan komentar-komentarnya tentang bagaimana afiliasi-afiliasi tersebut seharusnya mempengaruhi penilaian tentang transmisi mereka memberikan panduan penting bagi pengguna Mizan yang perlu membuat keputusan-keputusan tersebut sendiri dalam penelitian mereka.
